Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on the latest market reference we’re seeing at 6.38, I can offer supply at 6.75 per unit to start, which reflects current pricing and the value of locking in committed volume early. If you have a target volume and timing in mind, I’m open to discussing structure, but I’d need to stay around this level given market conditions. It is the opening turn and no buyer offer has been received, so the appropriate move is to anchor with an initial ask. The latest market-check is strong relative to my floor, so starting above the reference leaves room to negotiate while protecting margin and signaling that volume commitment matters.

Thanks for the opening proposal. That level is well above where we can buy and still make the downstream economics work, so I need to start materially lower at 2.32 per unit. If there’s flexibility tied to volume or scheduling, I’m willing to continue the discussion, but any deal would need to stay much closer to this range than to your current ask. The supplier’s 6.75 offer is far above my max buy threshold of 2.61, so acceptance is not viable. A low counteroffer preserves room to negotiate while clearly signaling a firm cost constraint.

I appreciate the counter, but 2.32 is too far below a workable supply price for us. The latest market reference we’re seeing is 11.15, so I’ve already moved substantially from my initial ask and can come down to 5.95 per unit as a serious concession. If you can commit volume and keep scheduling predictable, there may be some room to keep narrowing the gap, but we need to stay in a range that supports production and fulfillment. The buyer’s offer is above our reservation floor, but accepting it would leave too much value on the table given the observed market context. A counteroffer preserves the negotiation, signals flexibility, and anchors the discussion closer to a commercially reasonable level.

Thanks for the movement, but 5.95 per unit is still well outside the range we can justify on this buy. Our current ceiling is materially lower, and even allowing for the latest market reference, that number does not leave us enough downstream margin to proceed. If you’re able to move much closer to the low 2s, we can keep working toward a deal; otherwise we should pause here rather than force a price that won’t clear internally.
